source_paper_title: Load Balancing in Parallel Queues and Rank-Based Diffusions

Lastly, we note that although the case \(b = aK\) is not investigated here, we believe that the average long time behavior of the corresponding MJSQ system should resemble the unstable case (Theorem 3.7) where \(b > aK\). This is because, although the lowest queue will become empty infinitely often, the time spent at zero will be small and thus the average boundary effect of the origin will diminish over time.
title: Characterize long-time behavior of marginal join-the-shortest-queue at critical drift threshold
background_latex:
Consider the marginal join-the-shortest-queue (MJSQ) routing policy for a system of \(K\ge 2\) parallel single-server queues. Fix a heavy-traffic parameter \(n\in\mathbb N\) (large), and constants \(a\ge 0\) and \(b>0\). Each server works at exponential rate \(n\). Jobs arrive to a central dispatcher as a Poisson process of rate \(nK-\upsilon\sqrt n\), and are routed according to a rank-based rule that, in the MJSQ specialization, can be parameterized by drifts \(a_1=a-b\) and \(a_i=a\) for \(i=2,\dots,K\) (equivalently \(\upsilon=aK-b\)).

Let \(X_i^n(t)\) denote the length of the \(i\)-th shortest queue at time \(t\), with tie-breaking by lexicographic order, and define the diffusion-scaled ranked queue lengths \(\hat X_i^n(t)=X_i^n(t)/\sqrt n\). Define the diffusion-scaled gap process \(\hat Z^n(t)\in\mathbb R_+^K\) by
\[\hat Z_1^n(t)=\hat X_1^n(t),\qquad \hat Z_i^n(t)=\hat X_i^n(t)-\hat X_{i-1}^n(t),\quad i=2,\dots,K.\]
Write \(\hat Z_c^n(t)=(\hat Z_2^n(t),\dots,\hat Z_K^n(t))\in\mathbb R_+^{K-1}\) for the vector of all gaps except the first coordinate.

Under the heavy-traffic scaling \(n\to\infty\), the paper shows that when \(b<aK\) the full gap process admits a stationary distribution and when \(b>aK\) the full system is unstable but \(\hat Z_c^n(t)\) converges in distribution as \(t\to\infty\) (for each fixed large \(n\)), with an explicit limiting law after \(n\to\infty\). The parameter value \(b=aK\) is the critical threshold separating these regimes.
problem_definition_latex:
Determine the long-time (as \(t\to\infty\)) distributional behavior of the MJSQ diffusion-scaled gap process \(\hat Z^n(t)\) (or at least the reduced gap vector \(\hat Z_c^n(t)\)) in the critical parameter case
\[a_1=a-b,\; a_i=a\ (i=2,\dots,K),\qquad b=aK.\]
In particular, identify whether \(\hat Z_c^n(t)\) has a nontrivial limiting distribution as \(t\to\infty\) for large \(n\), and if so characterize its limit under the subsequent heavy-traffic limit \(n\to\infty\) (e.g., does it match the \(b>aK\) regime behavior).
latex:
Consider the marginal join-the-shortest-queue (MJSQ) routing policy for a system of \(K\ge 2\) parallel single-server queues. Fix a heavy-traffic parameter \(n\in\mathbb N\) (large), and constants \(a\ge 0\) and \(b>0\). Each server works at exponential rate \(n\). Jobs arrive to a central dispatcher as a Poisson process of rate \(nK-\upsilon\sqrt n\), and are routed according to a rank-based rule that, in the MJSQ specialization, can be parameterized by drifts \(a_1=a-b\) and \(a_i=a\) for \(i=2,\dots,K\) (equivalently \(\upsilon=aK-b\)).

Let \(X_i^n(t)\) denote the length of the \(i\)-th shortest queue at time \(t\), with tie-breaking by lexicographic order, and define the diffusion-scaled ranked queue lengths \(\hat X_i^n(t)=X_i^n(t)/\sqrt n\). Define the diffusion-scaled gap process \(\hat Z^n(t)\in\mathbb R_+^K\) by
\[\hat Z_1^n(t)=\hat X_1^n(t),\qquad \hat Z_i^n(t)=\hat X_i^n(t)-\hat X_{i-1}^n(t),\quad i=2,\dots,K.\]
Write \(\hat Z_c^n(t)=(\hat Z_2^n(t),\dots,\hat Z_K^n(t))\in\mathbb R_+^{K-1}\) for the vector of all gaps except the first coordinate.

Under the heavy-traffic scaling \(n\to\infty\), the paper shows that when \(b<aK\) the full gap process admits a stationary distribution and when \(b>aK\) the full system is unstable but \(\hat Z_c^n(t)\) converges in distribution as \(t\to\infty\) (for each fixed large \(n\)), with an explicit limiting law after \(n\to\infty\). The parameter value \(b=aK\) is the critical threshold separating these regimes.

Determine the long-time (as \(t\to\infty\)) distributional behavior of the MJSQ diffusion-scaled gap process \(\hat Z^n(t)\) (or at least the reduced gap vector \(\hat Z_c^n(t)\)) in the critical parameter case
\[a_1=a-b,\; a_i=a\ (i=2,\dots,K),\qquad b=aK.\]
In particular, identify whether \(\hat Z_c^n(t)\) has a nontrivial limiting distribution as \(t\to\infty\) for large \(n\), and if so characterize its limit under the subsequent heavy-traffic limit \(n\to\infty\) (e.g., does it match the \(b>aK\) regime behavior).

open_problem_quote: Lastly, we note that although the case \(b = aK\) is not investigated here, we believe that the average long time behavior of the corresponding MJSQ system should resemble the unstable case (Theorem 3.7) where \(b > aK\). This is because, although the lowest queue will become empty infinitely often, the time spent at zero will be small and thus the average boundary effect of the origin will diminish over time.
subject_classification: Stochastic networks and heavy-traffic limits (queueing theory)
counterexample_suitability_rationale: The question concerns a critical parameter regime and asymptotic long-time limits; while one can simulate finite \(n\) systems to probe behavior, disproving or confirming the conjectured resemblance to the \(b>aK\) regime is not a finite counterexample search with a crisp certificate.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: There is no clear automatically checkable fitness function for a proposed theorem/limit law at \(b=aK\); simulations could provide weak signals but would not reliably validate mathematical claims about limiting distributions and interchange of limits.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.
